_note: DRY content families for SA signs that are NOT bare codes in the official chart (sa_relevant was false). One Brave-grounded template per family, expanded per variant by scripts/signs/apply-external-drafts.mjs. Tokens: {v} {who} {vehicle} {condition} {restriction} {code}. Each family must cite a primary official source (SARTSM on transport.gov.za / NATIS).

braveQuery: South Africa road sign R201 minimum speed limit end of minimum speed K53 meaning
SARTSM regulatory signs R201 speed limit sign maximum speed South Africa learner licence meaning rule
snippetExcerpts: The R201 speed limit sign is a regulatory traffic sign that indicates the maximum speed allowed on a particular road.
an R Series regulatory sign used to indicate the maximum legal speed that may not be exceeded from the point of installation

template.plainEnglish: You may not drive faster than {v} km/h from this sign onwards. {v} km/h is the maximum allowed — not a speed you have to reach.
template.formalMeaning: Regulatory sign R201: the maximum speed limit on this section of road is {v} km/h. It applies from the sign until another speed-limit sign cancels or changes it.
template.behaviour: Be at or below {v} km/h by the time you reach the sign, and stay at or below {v} km/h until another speed sign changes the limit. Slow down further when conditions (rain, traffic, pedestrians) make {v} km/h unsafe.
template.commonMistake: Treating {v} km/h as the speed you must drive. It is the highest legal speed only — you may not exceed {v} km/h to keep up with other traffic, and you should go slower when it is not safe.
template.testHint: A red ring around a black number means MAXIMUM speed. Here the number is {v}, so {v} km/h is your ceiling.

braveQuery: South Africa road sign R101-600 end of minimum speed limit -600 de-restriction SARTSM
snippetExcerpts: A -600 suffix marks the END of the regulatory restriction shown, indicated by a diagonal stripe through the base sign.

template.plainEnglish: This sign marks the END of the {restriction}. From here, {ends}.
template.formalMeaning: Regulatory sign {code}: it cancels the {restriction} that applied on the road behind you.
template.commonMistake: Thinking the {restriction} still applies past this sign — it does not; the rule has ended here.
template.testHint: A regulatory sign with a diagonal stripe through it means the rule it shows has ENDED.

params.ends: there is no longer a minimum speed you must keep
params.behaviour: You may now travel slower than the previous minimum if needed, while still obeying any maximum speed limit and driving safely with the traffic flow.

params.ends: you are leaving the toll-road section
params.behaviour: No toll-route requirement applies beyond this point.

params.restriction: requirement to drive with headlamps on
params.ends: you no longer have to keep your headlamps switched on for this section
params.behaviour: You may switch your headlamps off — unless conditions such as dusk, rain, fog or a tunnel still require them by law.

params.ends: the weight limit no longer applies
params.behaviour: Vehicles that were excluded by the weight limit may proceed beyond this point.
Freeway rules (no pedestrians, no stopping, the freeway minimum speed) end here; normal road rules resume.
Freeway rules end here; normal road rules resume.
The special pedestrian-priority and walking-pace rules of a living street end here; normal road rules resume.

braveQuery: South Africa parking reservation sign R305 R301 P regulatory SARTSM reserved parking
snippetExcerpts: Parking reservation signs (the 'P' series) indicate that parking is reserved for the class of vehicle shown on the sign.

template.plainEnglish: This is a parking area reserved for {who}. Vehicles that are not {who} may not park here.
template.formalMeaning: Regulatory parking-reservation sign {code}: the parking here is reserved for {who}.
template.behaviour: Only park here if your vehicle is {who}. If it is not, park elsewhere.
template.commonMistake: Parking here when your vehicle does not qualify as {who}.
template.testHint: A 'P' on a blue sign with a vehicle symbol shows WHO may park — here, {who}.

overrides.plainEnglish: Parking is permitted in this area for all vehicles, unless another sign or road marking restricts it.
overrides.formalMeaning: Regulatory sign R305-P: parking is permitted here.
overrides.behaviour: You may park here. Obey any time limit, road markings or other parking signs that also apply.
overrides.commonMistake: Assuming you can park here without limit — a nearby plate may still set a time limit or conditions.
overrides.testHint: A plain blue 'P' sign means parking is permitted.

overrides.plainEnglish: Parking is permitted here, but only for up to 60 minutes.
overrides.formalMeaning: Regulatory sign R306-P: parking is permitted for a maximum of 60 minutes.
overrides.behaviour: You may park here for no longer than 60 minutes. Move your vehicle before the time is up.
overrides.commonMistake: Leaving your vehicle longer than 60 minutes.
overrides.testHint: A 'P' with '60 min' means parking is limited to 60 minutes.

braveQuery: South Africa road sign R242 motorcycles motorcars prohibited R245 axle massload prohibited SARTSM
snippetExcerpts: Prohibition signs use a red ring with a diagonal line; the symbol inside shows what is prohibited.

template.testHint: A red ring with a diagonal line means PROHIBITED — the symbol shows what.

params.plain: Motorcycles and motorcars may not enter or proceed past this sign.
params.formal: both motorcycles and motorcars are prohibited beyond this point.
params.behaviour: If you are riding a motorcycle or driving a motorcar, do not proceed; use a permitted route.
params.mistake: Assuming the ban is only for cars — it applies to motorcycles too.

params.plain: Vehicles with more than 12 tonnes on a tandem (bogie) axle may not proceed past this sign.
params.formal: vehicles whose tandem/bogie-axle mass-load exceeds 12 tonnes are prohibited.
params.behaviour: If your vehicle's tandem-axle load is over 12 t, do not proceed; use a permitted route.
params.mistake: Reading this as a total-vehicle weight limit — it is specifically the load on the tandem/bogie axle.

braveQuery: South Africa road sign R360 pedestrian crossing sign drivers yield to pedestrians K53
snippetExcerpts: Give priority to pedestrians crossing the road at a pedestrian crossing.

template.plainEnglish: There is a pedestrian crossing here. Pedestrians crossing have priority and you must give way to them.
template.formalMeaning: Sign {code}: marks a pedestrian crossing where pedestrians have right of way.
template.behaviour: Slow down on approach, be ready to stop, and yield to any pedestrian on or stepping onto the crossing. Do not overtake a vehicle stopped at the crossing.
template.commonMistake: Driving through because no one is on the crossing yet — you must yield to pedestrians already stepping on, and never overtake at a crossing.
template.testHint: Pedestrian-crossing sign = pedestrians first; be ready to stop and never overtake at the crossing.

braveQuery: South Africa road sign W365 moveable bridge ahead warning sign meaning
snippetExcerpts: Warning signs are triangular with a red border and warn of a hazard ahead.

template.plainEnglish: There is a moveable (opening) bridge ahead that may be raised or swung open for boats.
template.formalMeaning: Warning sign W365: a moveable bridge is ahead.
template.behaviour: Slow down and be prepared to stop — the bridge may be opening or closed to road traffic. Obey any signals or barriers at the bridge.
template.commonMistake: Treating it as an ordinary bridge and not expecting to stop while the bridge is open.
template.testHint: Red triangle with a bridge symbol = a moveable bridge ahead; be ready to stop.

braveQuery: South Africa road sign IN16 bus stop ahead IN17 modal transfer IN20 right of way over oncoming SARTSM guidance
snippetExcerpts: Information (IN) signs give the driver guidance information on a blue or green background.

params.plain: There is a bus stop ahead.
params.behaviour: Be aware that buses may be stopping or pulling away ahead; watch for passengers near the road.
params.mistake: Not expecting a bus to stop or pedestrians crossing near the bus stop.
params.hint: A bus symbol on an information sign means a bus stop ahead.

params.plain: There is a modal transfer point ahead — a place to change between transport modes (for example bus, taxi or train).
params.formal: indicates a modal transfer point ahead.
params.behaviour: Expect pedestrians and vehicles entering and leaving the transfer area; be ready for stopping traffic.
params.mistake: Not expecting extra pedestrian and stopping-vehicle activity around a transfer point.
params.hint: Modal transfer = a place to change transport modes; expect pedestrians and stopping traffic.

params.plain: You have right of way over oncoming vehicles at the narrow section ahead.
params.formal: indicates that you have priority over oncoming traffic at the narrow section ahead.
params.behaviour: You may proceed first, but still check that oncoming drivers have yielded before entering the narrow section.
params.mistake: Assuming priority means you need not look — always confirm oncoming traffic has actually yielded.
params.hint: Big arrow your way, small arrow against = YOU have priority over oncoming traffic.

braveQuery: South Africa road sign R325 bus stop sign reservation public transport SARTSM
snippetExcerpts: Bus-stop signs mark where buses stop to pick up and set down passengers.

template.plainEnglish: This marks a bus stop{detail}. Buses stop here to pick up and drop off passengers.
template.behaviour: Do not park or stop in the bus-stop zone. Watch for buses pulling in or out and for passengers near the road.
template.commonMistake: Parking or waiting in the bus-stop area, blocking buses.
template.testHint: Bus symbol = bus stop; keep the bus-stop zone clear.

params.detail: for authorised buses showing the route (line) number
for authorised buses showing the route (line) numbers

braveQuery: South Africa road sign R326 mini-bus stop sign taxi public transport SARTSM
snippetExcerpts: Mini-bus stop signs mark where mini-buses stop to pick up and set down passengers.

template.plainEnglish: This marks a mini-bus stop{detail}. Mini-buses stop here to pick up and drop off passengers.
template.behaviour: Do not park or stop in the mini-bus-stop zone. Watch for mini-buses pulling in or out and for passengers near the road.
template.commonMistake: Parking or waiting in the mini-bus-stop area, blocking mini-buses.
template.testHint: Mini-bus symbol = mini-bus stop; keep the zone clear.

params.detail: for authorised mini-buses showing the route (line) number
for authorised mini-buses showing the route (line) numbers
